name: Barranco de la Hoz Seca - Jaraba - Ibdes — circular desde el Monasterio de Piedra de Nuévalos
region_intro: Mountain biking around Nuévalos is characterized by its diverse terrain within the province of Zaragoza, Aragon. The region features dramatic geological formations, including the verdant Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, where the Piedra River carves canyons and creates numerous waterfalls. This area offers a striking contrast of lush greenery against the surrounding arid landscapes, providing varied environments for mountain bike trails. The routes often navigate through the foothills of the Iberian System Mountains, presenting a mix of elevations and technical challenges. Best mountain bike trails around Nuévalos The most popular mountain bike trail is Barranco de la Hoz Seca - Jaraba - Ibdes — circular desde el Monasterio de Piedra de Nuévalos, a 22.1 miles (35.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging ride through varied landscapes, starting from the Monasterio de Piedra. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ateca – Calatayud loop from Munébrega, a moderate 28.2 miles (45.4 km) path. This route features a mix of forest tracks and open terrain with significant elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the La Caprichosa Waterfall of Ibdes – La Paradera Waterfall loop from Nuévalos, a 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail leading through scenic areas with waterfalls, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Nuévalos is defined by dramatic geological formations, verdant canyons, and the foothills of the Iberian System Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging, technical routes. The routes in Nuévalos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Nuévalos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Nuévalos?
A: There are 12 mountain bike trails around Nuévalos listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences from easy to difficult.

Q: How challenging are the mountain bike trails around Nuévalos?
A: The trails around Nuévalos offer a mix of difficulties. You'll find 1 easy route, 10 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route. The terrain varies from forest tracks to more technical sections, especially in the foothills of the Iberian System Mountains.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking near Nuévalos?
A: Many routes pass through or near the stunning Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, renowned for its waterfalls and canyons. You can encounter features like the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5119833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirror Lake (Lago del Espejo)&lt;/a&gt;. The region's dramatic geological formations and lush greenery provide a picturesque backdrop for your ride.

Q: Are there routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, several routes allow you to experience the region's beautiful waterfalls. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall of Ibdes – La Paradera Waterfall loop from Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.0-mile (19.3 km) trail specifically designed to lead through scenic areas with waterfalls.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Nuévalos are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A notable example is the challenging 22.1-mile (35.6 km) route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986820919/barranco-de-la-hoz-seca-jaraba-ibdes-circular-desde-el-monasterio-de-piedra-de-nuevalos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Hoz Seca - Jaraba - Ibdes — circular desde el Monasterio de Piedra de Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Nuévalos?
A: Nuévalos and the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park are appealing throughout the year. Each season offers unique beauty, from vibrant spring blooms to autumnal hues. The park's microclimate, with its abundant water features, can offer a refreshing environment even in summer. However, spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for mountain biking.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Nuévalos?
A: While many routes offer varied terrain, there is at least one easy route available, such as the 8.2-mile (13.2 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt;. These easier options are generally suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails around Nuévalos?
A: The Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, a central feature of the region, has specific regulations regarding pets. While some areas might be accessible, it's always best to check the specific rules for each trail or park section you plan to visit to ensure dogs are permitted and if they need to be on a leash.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Nuévalos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the dramatic geological formations, and the stunning natural beauty, especially the verdant Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park with its numerous waterfalls.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails?
A: Nuévalos and the surrounding towns offer various options for accommodation and dining. Given the popularity of the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, you'll find facilities catering to visitors, including cafes, restaurants, and hotels, making it convenient to refuel and relax after your ride.

Q: Where can I park when visiting mountain bike trails in Nuévalos?
A: Parking is generally available at key access points for popular trails, particularly near the Monasterio de Piedra and in the town of Nuévalos itself. It's advisable to check specific starting points for routes on komoot for detailed parking information.

Q: Can I reach the mountain bike trails in Nuévalos by public transport?
A: While Nuévalos is accessible by public transport, options might be limited, especially for reaching specific trailheads directly. It's recommended to research bus schedules to Nuévalos or nearby towns and consider the distance to your desired starting point. Having a car often provides more flexibility for exploring the region's diverse trails.
tips: We are in the Monasterio de la Piedra Natural Park (not free), an idyllic place for nature lovers where we will find waterfalls, lakes, caves, beautiful walkways and more. The building itself is an old restored Cistercian monastery from the 13th century, where we can see the transition from Romanesque to Cistercian Gothic and where its spectacular cloister stands out. It is important to check prices and schedules on the web: monasteriopiedra.com
Imposing and impressive hermitage located in the Barranco de la Hoz Seca, with rocky walls next to the river canyon. A highly recommended visit if we are in the area. The guided tour is recommended.

Beautiful waterfall located in the town of Ibdes. A very cozy corner without coverage to enjoy nature to the fullest like the sound of falling water. We will find a picnic area
Waterfall integrated into the Ibdes water route.
